title: Calea caleoides
uses: Calea caleoides, a plant native to Central and South America, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential therapeutic properties. Its leaves and stems contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. Some studies suggest that Calea caleoides may have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, wounds, and digestive problems. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Calea caleoides.
taxonomy: Calea caleoides is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It belongs to the genus Calea, which includes plants native to tropical America. Calea caleoides is classified in the kingdom Plantae, and its taxonomy is further defined by its family, genus, and species levels. At the family level, Calea caleoides is part of the large and diverse Asteraceae family. intro: Calea caleoides, also known as Mexican Damiana or Desert Marigold, is a shrub-like perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The health benefits of Calea caleoides include its potential to alleviate anxiety and stress, as well as its ability to act as a natural sedative and aphrodisiac. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Calea caleoides include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation and reduce symptoms of insomnia and restlessness.
